title: CSS Intro
content:
CSS Introduction

What is CSS?

CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. It is a language used to control the presentation, layout, and styling of HTML elements on a webpage. CSS allows you to define colors, fonts, spacing, layout, and other visual improvements.

CSS Syntax

A CSS rule consists of a selector and a declaration block. The selector targets HTML elements, and the declaration block contains property:value pairs.

Example
selector {
  property: value;
  property: value;
}

title: CSS Selectors
content:
CSS Selectors

What Are CSS Selectors?

CSS selectors are patterns used to select the HTML elements you want to style. Common types include element selectors, class selectors, and ID selectors.

Basic Selector Example

This rule selects all <p> elements, sets text color to red and centers the text.

Example
p {
  color: red;
  text-align: center;
}

title: Adding Styles
content:
Adding CSS to HTML

How to Add CSS

CSS can be added to HTML in three ways: inline, internal, or external. Inline uses the style attribute. Internal uses the <style> element inside the <head>. External uses a linked stylesheet file.

Inline CSS Example
<h1 style="color:blue;">Hello World</h1>

title: Colors & Backgrounds
content:
CSS Colors & Backgrounds

Text and Background Colors

CSS uses properties such as color and background-color to change text and background colors. Values can be color names, HEX codes, RGB, or RGBA.

Color Example
<style>
h1 {
  color: white;
  background-color: blue;
}
p {
  color: red;
}
</style>

<h1>Header</h1>
<p>Paragraph text</p>

title: Margins & Padding
content:
CSS Margins & Padding

The CSS Box Model

All HTML elements can be considered as boxes. Margins and padding dictate the spacing in and around these boxes.

Margins

The margin property controls the space outside an element’s border. Values can be set for all sides or each side individually.

Margin Example
<style>
.box {
  margin: 20px;
  background-color: lightblue;
}
</style>
<div class="box">I have a margin of 20px!</div>

Padding

The padding property controls the space inside an element’s border, between the border and the content.

Padding Example
<style>
.box {
  padding: 20px;
  border: 1px solid black;
}
</style>
<div class="box">I have 20px of padding inside!</div>

title: CSS Advanced Selectors
content:
CSS Selectors (Advanced)

What Are CSS Selectors?

CSS selectors are patterns used to select the HTML elements you want to style. They include element selectors, class selectors, id selectors, and more.

Grouping Selectors

You can group multiple selectors to apply the same styles to different elements by separating them with commas.

Grouping Example
<style>
h1, h2, p {
  color: blue;
  text-align: center;
}
</style>

<h1>Heading 1</h1>
<h2>Heading 2</h2>
<p>This is a paragraph.</p>

Attribute Selectors

Attribute selectors let you style elements with specific attributes or attribute values.

Attribute Selector Example
<style>
a[target="_blank"] {
  background-color: yellow;
}
</style>

<a href="#" target="_blank">This link opens in a new window (Yellow)</a><br><br>
<a href="#">This is a normal link</a>

title: CSS Box Model
content:
CSS Box Model

Understanding the Box Model

Every HTML element is treated as a rectangular box. The CSS box model consists of content, padding, border, and margin. This model helps you control spacing and layout precisely.



Box Example

Box Model Example
<style>
.box {
  width: 200px;
  padding: 10px;
  border: 5px solid black;
  margin: 20px;
  background-color: lightgreen;
}
</style>

<div class="box">I am a box! Notice the space inside (padding) and outside (margin) my borders.</div>

title: CSS Positioning
content:
CSS Positioning

What is Positioning?

The CSS position property lets you control how elements are placed on the page. You can override the normal flow of the document using positioning types like static, relative, absolute, fixed, and sticky.



Positioning Example

Absolute Position Example
<style>
.container {
  position: relative;
  height: 150px;
  background-color: #f1f5f9;
  border: 2px solid #cbd5e1;
}
.box {
  position: absolute;
  top: 30px;
  left: 50px;
  background-color: lightcoral;
  padding: 15px;
  color: white;
}
</style>

<div class="container">
  <div class="box">I am absolutely positioned!</div>
</div>

title: CSS Flexbox
content:
CSS Flexbox

What is Flexbox?

CSS Flexbox (Flexible Box Layout) is a layout model that makes it easier to align and distribute space among items in a container, even when their size is unknown or dynamic.



Flex Container Example

Flexbox Layout
<style>
.flex-container {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-around;
  background-color: #1e293b;
  padding: 20px;
}
.flex-item {
  background-color: #00ffb3;
  padding: 20px;
  font-weight: bold;
}
</style>

<div class="flex-container">
  <div class="flex-item">Item 1</div>
  <div class="flex-item">Item 2</div>
  <div class="flex-item">Item 3</div>
</div>

title: CSS Grid Layout
content:
CSS Grid Layout

What is CSS Grid?

CSS Grid is a powerful two-dimensional layout system that allows you to define rows and columns, then place elements into grid cells. It helps design responsive layouts without using floats.



Grid Container Example

Grid Layout Example
<style>
.grid-container {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: auto auto auto;
  gap: 10px;
  background-color: #1e293b;
  padding: 10px;
}
.grid-item {
  background-color: #cbd5e1;
  padding: 20px;
  text-align: center;
}
</style>

<div class="grid-container">
  <div class="grid-item">1</div>
  <div class="grid-item">2</div>
  <div class="grid-item">3</div>
  <div class="grid-item">4</div>
  <div class="grid-item">5</div>
  <div class="grid-item">6</div>
</div>

title: CSS Media Queries
content:
CSS Media Queries

Responsive Design

CSS media queries allow you to apply styles based on device characteristics such as screen width. They are essential for building responsive web pages that adapt to desktops, tablets, and phones.

Media Query Example

Media Queries Syntax
<style>
body {
  background-color: lightgreen;
  text-align: center;
  padding: 50px;
}

/* If the screen is 600px or smaller, change the background color to lightblue */
@media screen and (max-width: 600px) {
  body {
    background-color: lightblue;
  }
}
</style>

<h1>Resize the preview window!</h1>
<p>The background is lightgreen on wide screens, but turns lightblue on small screens.</p>

title: CSS Transitions
content:
CSS Transitions

What Are Transitions?

CSS transitions allow you to smoothly change property values over a given duration. They are often used for hover effects and interactive UI feedback.

Basic Transition Example

Transition on Hover
<style>
.box {
  width: 100px;
  height: 100px;
  background-color: red;
  transition: background-color 2s, transform 1s;
}
.box:hover {
  background-color: blue;
  transform: rotate(45deg);
}
</style>

<div class="box">Hover me!</div>

title: CSS Hover & Effects
content:
CSS Hover & Effects

:hover Pseudo-class

The :hover pseudo-class applies styles when the user moves the mouse over an element — useful for interactive effects on links, buttons, or images.

Hover Example

Text Hover Color Change
<style>
.hover-btn {
  padding: 10px 20px;
  background-color: lightgray;
  color: black;
  text-decoration: none;
  font-family: sans-serif;
  display: inline-block;
}
.hover-btn:hover {
  color: orange;
  background-color: #333;
}
</style>

<a href="#" class="hover-btn">Hover over this link</a>

title: CSS Box Sizing
content:
CSS Box Sizing

What Is Box Sizing?

The box-sizing property defines how the width and height of an element are calculated — whether padding and border are included in the total width or not.



Box Sizing Example

Box Sizing Demo
<style>
.my-box {
  width: 300px;
  padding: 50px;
  border: 5px solid black;
  box-sizing: border-box;
  background-color: lightyellow;
}
</style>

<div class="my-box">Because of border-box, my total width stays exactly 300px, even with padding!</div>

title: CSS Box Shadow
content:
CSS Box Shadow

Applying Shadows

The box-shadow property adds shadow effects to elements. You can set horizontal and vertical offsets, blur radius, spread, color, and even inset shadows.

Box Shadow Example

Simple Box Shadow
<style>
.shadow-box {
  width: 200px;
  height: 100px;
  background-color: white;
  box-shadow: 10px 10px 15px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
  padding: 20px;
  margin: 30px;
  border-radius: 8px;
}
</style>

<div class="shadow-box">I have a cool, soft shadow!</div>

title: CSS Animations
content:
CSS Animations

What Are Animations?

CSS animations let an element gradually change from one style to another. You define keyframes to control the animation sequence.

Animation Example

Move Animation
<style>
@keyframes slideRight {
  from {left: 0px; background-color: red;}
  to {left: 200px; background-color: yellow;}
}
.animated-box {
  width: 100px;
  height: 100px;
  position: relative;
  animation: slideRight 2s infinite alternate;
  border-radius: 50%;
}
</style>

<div class="animated-box"></div>

title: CSS Transforms
content:
CSS Transforms

What Are CSS Transforms?

The CSS transform property allows you to rotate, scale, skew, or move elements in 2D or 3D space without affecting the normal document flow. It’s commonly used to create dynamic, interactive effects.



Basic Transform Example

Transform Rotate
<style>
.transform-box {
  width: 100px;
  height: 100px;
  background-color: lightcoral;
  color: white;
  text-align: center;
  line-height: 100px;
  margin: 50px;
  transform: rotate(45deg);
}
</style>

<div class="transform-box">Rotated!</div>

title: CSS Borders
content:
CSS Borders

Understanding Borders

CSS border properties let you define the look of an element’s border, including style, width, and color. You can create solid, dashed, dotted, double, groove, ridge, inset, or outset borders.



Border Example

Dotted Border
<style>
.border-box {
  border: 4px dotted blue;
  padding: 20px;
  background-color: #f8fafc;
  font-size: 18px;
  text-align: center;
}
</style>

<div class="border-box">I have a dotted blue border!</div>

title: CSS Backgrounds
content:
CSS Backgrounds

Using Backgrounds

The CSS background property lets you set background colors, images, sizes, positions, and more for an element. It’s a powerful shorthand for multiple background properties.

Background Example

Background Image
<style>
.bg-box {
  height: 200px;
  /* Using a placeholder image for the demo */
  background: url('https://picsum.photos/400/200') no-repeat center/cover;
  color: white;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  font-size: 24px;
  font-weight: bold;
  text-shadow: 2px 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.8);
}
</style>

<div class="bg-box">Beautiful Background</div>

title: CSS Typography
content:
CSS Typography & Fonts

Font Families

Use the CSS font-family property to set which font your text should use. Always include fallback fonts to ensure text displays even if one font isn’t available.

Custom Fonts (@font-face)

The @font-face rule allows you to load custom fonts from external files so that visitors don’t need them installed locally.

Typography Example
<style>
.custom-text {
  font-family: 'Courier New', Courier, monospace;
  font-size: 24px;
  color: #1e293b;
  letter-spacing: 2px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  border-left: 5px solid #00ffb3;
  padding-left: 15px;
}
</style>

<p class="custom-text">Custom Typography Box</p>

title: CSS Variables
content:
CSS Variables

What Are CSS Variables?

CSS variables (custom properties) let you store values that can be reused throughout your stylesheet. They help make your code easier to maintain and update. Declare them with two dashes (--) and use them with the var() function.

CSS Variables Example

Using Custom Properties
<style>
:root {
  --main-bg: #e0f2fe;
  --main-color: #0369a1;
  --padding-large: 20px;
}

.theme-box {
  background-color: var(--main-bg);
  color: var(--main-color);
  padding: var(--padding-large);
  border: 2px solid var(--main-color);
  text-align: center;
  font-family: sans-serif;
  font-weight: bold;
}
</style>

<div class="theme-box">I am styled using CSS Variables!</div>

title: CSS Text Effects
content:
CSS Text Shadow & Decoration

Text Shadow

The text-shadow property adds a shadow to text. You can control the horizontal offset, vertical offset, blur radius, and color.

Text Shadow Example
<style>
h1 {
  text-shadow: 3px 3px 5px rgba(0,0,0,0.5);
  color: #3b82f6;
  font-family: sans-serif;
}
</style>

<h1>Shadow Text</h1>

Text Decoration

CSS lets you decorate text with underlines, overlines, and line styles using properties like text-decoration-line, text-decoration-color, and text-decoration-style.

Text Decoration Example
<style>
.fancy-link {
  font-size: 24px;
  text-decoration-line: underline overline;
  text-decoration-color: red;
  text-decoration-style: wavy;
}
</style>

<p class="fancy-link">Super Fancy Text!</p>

title: CSS Filters
content:
CSS Filters

Visual Effects with Filters

The filter property applies graphical effects like blur, brightness, contrast, grayscale, and drop shadows to elements (usually images). Filters do not change layout but heavily affect how the element looks.



Filter Example

Blur and Brightness
<style>
.filtered-img {
  filter: blur(2px) brightness(130%) sepia(50%);
  border-radius: 8px;
  width: 100%;
  max-width: 300px;
}
</style>

<h3>Filtered Image:</h3>
<img src="https://picsum.photos/400/300" alt="Random" class="filtered-img">

title: CSS Blend Modes
content:
CSS Blend Modes

Blend Mode Basics

The mix-blend-mode property determines how the content of an element blends with the background behind it, similar to image editing blend modes like multiply, screen, and overlay.



Blend Mode Example

Mix Blend Mode
<style>
.blend-container {
  background-color: lightcoral;
  padding: 20px;
  display: inline-block;
}

.blend-img {
  mix-blend-mode: multiply;
  width: 200px;
  border-radius: 50%;
}
</style>

<div class="blend-container">
  <img src="https://picsum.photos/200/200" alt="Blend" class="blend-img">
</div>

title: CSS Text Align & Transform
content:
CSS Text Alignment & Transform

Text Alignment

The text-align property specifies the horizontal alignment of text inside an element. You can align text to the left, right, center, or justify it across the width.



Text Align Example
<style>
.center-text {
  text-align: center;
  color: #0284c7;
}
</style>

<h2 class="center-text">I am perfectly centered!</h2>

Text Transform

The text-transform property controls the capitalization of text. You can make text uppercase, lowercase, or capitalize the first letter of each word.

Text Transform Example
<style>
.uppercase {
  text-transform: uppercase;
}
.capitalize {
  text-transform: capitalize;
}
</style>

<p class="uppercase">this text is forced to uppercase.</p>
<p class="capitalize">this text has capitalized words.</p>

title: CSS Text Spacing
content:
CSS Text Spacing & Indentation

Text Indentation

The text-indent property specifies the indentation of the first line in a text block. It can accept positive and negative values.

Letter & Word Spacing

Use letter-spacing to increase or decrease the space between characters, and word-spacing to adjust the space between words.



Spacing Example
<style>
.spaced-letters {
  letter-spacing: 5px;
  color: #b91c1c;
}
.spaced-words {
  word-spacing: 15px;
}
.indented {
  text-indent: 50px;
}
</style>

<h3 class="spaced-letters">Spaced Letters</h3>
<p class="spaced-words">This paragraph has wide spaces between words.</p>
<p class="indented">This paragraph has an indented first line, exactly like you would see in a printed book or newspaper article.</p>

title: CSS Text Decoration
content:
CSS Text Decoration & Shadow

Text Decoration

The text-decoration property controls decorative lines on text, like underline, overline, and line-through. You can also specify color, style, and thickness.

Text Decoration Example
<style>
.fancy-text {
  text-decoration-line: underline overline;
  text-decoration-color: red;
  text-decoration-style: wavy;
  font-size: 20px;
}
</style>

<p class="fancy-text">Wavy decorated text!</p>

Text Shadow

The text-shadow property adds shadows to text. You can define horizontal and vertical offsets, blur, and color. Multiple shadows can be applied at once.

Text Shadow Example
<style>
.shadow-text {
  text-shadow: 2px 2px 5px rgba(0,0,0,0.4);
  font-size: 30px;
  color: #0ea5e9;
}
</style>

<h1 class="shadow-text">Text with Shadow</h1>

title: CSS Text Overflow
content:
CSS Text Overflow

What Is Text Overflow?

The text-overflow property defines how overflowed text that is not displayed should be handled. It can be clipped or shown with an ellipsis (...). Both white-space: nowrap and overflow: hidden are required for it to work properly.



Text Overflow Example

Clip and Ellipsis
<style>
.ellipsis-box {
  width: 200px;
  border: 2px solid #1e293b;
  padding: 10px;
  background-color: #f1f5f9;
  /* The magic trio for text overflow: */
  white-space: nowrap;
  overflow: hidden;
  text-overflow: ellipsis;
}
</style>

<div class="ellipsis-box">
  This is some very long text that will definitely not fit inside this 200px box!
</div>

title: CSS Word Wrap
content:
CSS Word Wrap & Break

Handling Long Words

Use word-wrap (or its modern alias overflow-wrap) to allow long words to break and wrap onto the next line if needed. The word-break property controls how words break when they reach the end of a line.



Word Wrap & Break Example

Wrap and Break Text
<style>
.wrap-box {
  width: 150px;
  border: 2px solid #0284c7;
  padding: 10px;
  background-color: #e0f2fe;
  word-wrap: break-word;
}
</style>

<p class="wrap-box">
  This paragraph contains a reallylooooooooooooooooooooooooongword that will break to the next line instead of overflowing out of the box!
</p>

title: CSS Writing Modes
content:
CSS Writing Modes

Text Orientation and Layout

The writing-mode property defines whether text flows horizontally or vertically. For example, horizontal-tb makes text flow left-to-right (default), while vertical-rl makes text flow top-to-bottom with right-to-left horizontal progression.



Writing Mode Example

Vertical Text
<style>
p.vertical {
  writing-mode: vertical-rl;
  background-color: #10b981;
  color: white;
  padding: 15px;
  height: 200px;
  font-size: 18px;
  font-weight: bold;
}
</style>

<p class="vertical">
  This text flows from top to bottom!
</p>

title: Course Summary
content:
CSS Complete Course Summary 🎨

You Did It! 🎉

You have successfully completed the CSS course! You now have the power to take plain, boring HTML and transform it into stunning, responsive, and interactive web experiences.

  • Core Fundamentals: Selectors, Colors, Backgrounds, and Typography.
  • The Box Model: Margins, Padding, Borders, and Box-Sizing.
  • Modern Layouts: Flexbox, CSS Grid, Positioning, and Responsive Design (Media Queries).
  • Visual Effects: Transforms, Transitions, Keyframe Animations, Shadows, and Filters.

Your websites look amazing! Now, it's time to make them think and react. Let's dive into JavaScript!
